tool.name: splitsource
tool.summary: Split annotated records into one fragment per simple source feature

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: `splitsource` divides one annotated sequence record into multiple fragments using `source` features as the split plan. EMBOSS-RS v1 treats only simple single-span `source` features as eligible, requires at least two such features per record, and emits one unannotated fragment sequence per source interval in source order.

sections.title: Inputs
sections.content: The current interface accepts one annotated EMBL or GenBank input path. Records must contain at least two eligible `source` features, and each eligible source feature must have a simple single-span location.

sections.title: Outputs
sections.content: The tool emits a sequence collection through the shared FASTA output path. Fragment identifiers are derived as `<parent>-source-<n>`, descriptions record the ordinal and interval used, and CLI summaries report the input path, fragment count, and the conservative source-feature selection rule.

sections.title: Current Status
sections.content: This method is implemented and exposed through `emboss-rs splitsource`. Validation currently covers deterministic splitting of a committed GenBank fixture containing two `source` features, along with rejection of records that do not contain multiple eligible source spans.

sections.title: Caveats
sections.content: The first release does not preserve annotation on emitted fragments, does not support complex `source` feature locations, and does not merge or infer fragment metadata beyond conservative identifier and description derivation.

artifacts.label: Annotated GenBank source fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed annotated GenBank fixture containing two simple `source` features for deterministic fragment emission.

examples.title: Emit one fragment per simple source feature
examples.description: Runs `splitsource` against a committed annotated GenBank fixture and emits two unannotated FASTA fragments in source-feature order.

expected_outputs.label: Source-derived FASTA fragments
expected_outputs.description: A FASTA sequence collection containing two fragments with residues `AAAT` and `GGGC`.
